data.title: Elevate invests in Gonzalez Advisors in Southern California, expanding its national presence. Led by Managing Partner John Gonzalez.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, in Los Angeles, Elevate, a nationwide accounting and advisory platform supported by significant long-term growth capital, revealed a vital investment in Gonzalez Advisors. This firm, situated in Orange County and spearheaded by Managing Partner John Gonzalez, specializes in tax and advisory services.

data.title: FLSmidth reports ongoing transactions as part of share buy-back program.
data.description: According to recent data, the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 4.2%, marking a significant drop from the previous month's 5%. This decrease represents over 1.5 million individuals who have found employment opportunities. Additionally, the GDP growth for the quarter has been reported at 6.7%, surpassing previous estimates and signaling a strong economic recovery. The stock market has also seen positive trends, with the main index reaching a record high of 35,000 points. Experts attribute these positive developments to various factors, including government stimulus packages and increased consumer spending.Despite these improvements, concerns linger over rising inflation, which has climbed to 3.5% due to increased demand and supply chain disruptions. Despite these challenges, the overall economic outlook remains optimistic as the country continues to navigate its recovery from the effects of the pandemic.

data.title: Alcoa and Australian government partner to modernize approvals for mining activities in Western Australia under EPBC Act.
data.description: Alcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) has revealed plans to modernize its Western Australian mining operations. The company's Australian branch, Alcoa of Australia, will conduct a Strategic Assessment under Australia’s federal EPBC Act, extending to 2045. This agreement with the Australian Federal Government aims to streamline the approvals process for mining activities. The assessment will cover both existing and future mining areas, ensuring compliance with environmental regulations. This move signifies a step towards sustainable mining practices in Australia.

data.title: Getty Realty Corp. priced public offering of 4M shares of common stock for $131M, with option for 600K more shares.
data.description: Getty Realty Corp, a net lease REIT, disclosed the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 4,000,000 shares of common stock on a forward basis, generating around $131 million in gross proceeds. Additionally, the company and the forward purchasers have given the underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 600,000 more shares of common stock. The offering is set to conclude on February 19, 2026, subject to standard closing conditions.
U.S. is taking a multipronged approach to curbing its dependence on China for critical minerals, but it could take years to see results-NA

data.title: Cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin slide before Fed meeting. Bitcoin at $67,011.90 (-2.66%) and Ethereum at $1,973.18 (-1.34%). $200 million liquidated in last 24 hours. Bullish sentiment for Bitcoin persist.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies experienced losses, with Bitcoin dropping by 2.66% to $67,011.90, Ethereum down by 1.34% at $1,973.18, XRP falling by 1.18% to $1.46, Solana decreasing by 3.00% to $84.33, and Dogecoin slipping by 0.14% to $0.1006. Bitcoin hit a low of $66,600 while trading volumes rose. Ethereum struggled at $2,000, XRP and Solana saw declines. Cryptocurrency-related stocks like Strategy Inc. and BitMine Immersion Technologies, Inc. fell by 3.89% and 3.86% respectively. $200 million was liquidated in the market in the last 24 hours, with $138 million from long liquidations alone. Bitcoin's open interest rose by 0.68%, indicating new short positions. Investors on Binance, however, remained bullish on Bitcoin, with more long positions over shorts.

data.title: Two ETF issuers, Bitwise and GraniteShares, have filed for 6 US election prediction market ETFs, expanding investment opportunities.
data.description: Two additional companies have submitted legal documents for approval for the creation of six exchange-traded funds (ETFs) related to predicting US election results. These ETFs provide investors with opportunities to invest in and profit from potential election outcomes.

data.title: Portland General Electric Co. prices public offering of 9,467,455 shares of common stock. ($POR) #PGE #stockofferings
data.description: Today, Portland General Electric Company (NYSE: POR) based in Portland, Oregon disclosed the pricing details of its underwritten public offering. The offering consists of 9,467,455 shares of its common stock. This integrated energy company made the announcement, stating that all the shares are being offered in this public offering.

data.title: Coin Center advises Senate against eliminating bill protecting cryptocurrency developers. Internet & cloud providers not penalized if misused.
data.description: According to Coin Center, internet and cloud hosting providers are not held liable for criminal activities on their platforms. The organization argues that just like these providers, cryptocurrency developers should not be prosecuted for misuse of their technology.

data.title: Michael Saylor sees current crypto winter as mild, predicts spring ahead followed by 'glorious' summer. Bitcoin bull feels confident due to institutional backing, expects shorter winter.
data.description: Chairman Michael Saylor of Strategy Inc. (NASDAQ: MSTR) optimistically stated that the current "crypto winter" is less severe than previous ones and will lead into a promising spring and summer. Saylor expressed his confidence during an interview with Fox Business, noting that this winter will be shorter and less impactful. He highlighted the growing institutional support for Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) by entities such as banks. Saylor's positive outlook on the market suggests a bullish trend ahead and emphasizes Bitcoin's dominance despite the ongoing challenges. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: New working group led by The Digital Chamber seeks clarity on prediction market regulations, urges CFTC to oversee.
data.description: The Digital Chamber has initiated the Prediction Markets Working Group to uphold the sector’s principles and push for the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to retain primary regulatory control. This group will focus on promoting the industry's core beliefs and aims to advocate effectively for maintaining oversight within CFTC's jurisdiction.
